content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments identify genuine, verified defects. Amendment 1 is a clear testability violation with a hardcoded filesystem adapter. Amendment 2 exposes an overly broad filter that causes duplicate verification of None-run_id mappings across runs, compounded by journal rebuild always producing unverified state. Amendment 3 correctly identifies that stale planned-elsewhere findings from superseded completion rounds persist and get verified because the identity key lacks round/rollback provenance. Amendment 4 identifies a fundamental backward-compatibility gap: the Unknown variant only protects new code (forward compatibility), while hard rollback restores old code that lacks both the new event type and the catch-all, making journals unreadable.

## Votes

- `fr-14-1378ea30`: Accept
  Verified: rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs:2429-2436) imports and directly instantiates FsMilestoneJournalStore, bypassing the MilestoneJournalPort trait that exists at service.rs:74. load_planned_elsewhere_mappings (service.rs:2416-2422) accepts _mapping_store with the unused-parameter underscore prefix. Meanwhile, record_planned_elsewhere_mapping correctly accepts &impl MilestoneJournalPort, demonstrating the intended pattern. The fix is mechanical — thread a &impl MilestoneJournalPort parameter through — and the inconsistency is a real testability barrier.
- `fr-14-0b26b2bc`: Accept
  Verified: the filter at success_reconciliation.rs:722 uses is_none_or which matches mappings with run_id: None on every run. The secondary guard !m.mapped_bead_verified at line 743 appears protective, but it is ineffective across runs because journal rebuild (rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal) always produces mappings with mapped_bead_verified: false — verification status is not persisted back to the journal. So on each new run: journal rebuild → all mappings unverified → None-run_id mappings pass both filters → duplicate br comment calls. Scoping to run_id == Some(current_run_id) is the correct fix and makes the intent explicit.
- `fr-14-48dbcac3`: Accept
  Verified: PlannedElsewhereMapping (model.rs:736-751) carries only run_id with no completion_round or rollback_count fields, while the engine tracks both for artifact naming (engine.rs:7379-7397). The identity key for dedup is (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) — no provenance fields. Mappings are written eagerly after stage commit (engine.rs:3082-3092). If round 1 produces a mapping and round 2's final-review no longer accepts that finding, the round 1 mapping persists (nothing to supersede it since the same identity key isn't reproduced), and success reconciliation picks it up because run_id matches. This leads to verifying and commenting on a finding the latest review rejected.
- `fr-14-6ceff67a`: Accept
  Verified: the Unknown variant with #[serde(other)] at model.rs:296-301 provides forward compatibility only — new code gracefully handles unknown event types. But hard rollback (service.rs:1793-1810) executes git reset --hard to an older SHA whose MilestoneEventType enum lacks both PlannedElsewhereMapped and Unknown. That old code's serde_json::from_str at fs.rs:3064-3082 will fail with 'unknown variant planned_elsewhere_mapped' since there is no catch-all. Once a single planned_elsewhere_mapped event is appended to the journal, all subsequent hard rollbacks to pre-feature commits make the journal unreadable. This requires a write-compatibility strategy (separate file, version marker, or deferred writes) rather than a read-compatibility patch in new code.
